DISCOVERY 302, THE COURT MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF TAMAYA GARCIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF TAMAYA GARCIAS SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, AND FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE AND FOR AN AWARD OF SANCTIONS. MOTION IS DENIED AS MOOT. SANCTIONS ARE GRANTED AND DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO PAY $1,200 TO PARTIALLY COMPENSATE PLAINTIFF FOR THE WORK REQUIRED TO BRING THIS MOTION. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER) JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Plaintiff Tamaya Garcias Special Interrogatories, Set One, Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Form Interrogatories, Set One And For An Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Brian Brophy, Esq., 415-504-2165, appearing for Plaintiff;
No other appearance.

The Court modifies its tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TAMAYA GARCIA'S Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Plaintiff Tamaya Garcias Special Interrogatories, Set One, Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Form Interrogatories, Set One And For An Award Of Sanctions.

Based on the Notice of Non-Receipt dated March 13, 2019, it appears that Defendants provided responses to the subject discovery requests, albeit after they were due. Therefore the motion to compel responses to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Requests for Production is DENIED AS MOOT. With respect to the request for sanctions, CRC 3.1348(a) permits an award of sanctions even if discovery responses are provided after a motion to compel is filed, and CRC 3.1348(b) provides that failure to oppose a motion does not operate as a concession of the propriety of a sanction. The meet-and-confer correspondence indicates late discovery of issues and some effort to resolve the dispute without court intervention. Nevertheless Defendants did not provide responses until after their requested extension. Balancing these considerations, Sanctions are GRANTED and Defendants are ordered to pay $1,200 to partially compensate Plaintiff for the work required to bring this motion.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
